data.description:
# Workflow-engine remediation: unify LLM functionality on the engine, restore visual editing, document intended use

## Context

Gaps surfaced while finalizing the plan-review gate (epic `5fd2`). This epic CAPTURES scope + initial
concerns; a dedicated brainstorm session does the deep design BEFORE implementation (per `joe-brainstorm`).

1. **No usage documentation.** The workflow engine (epic `a88f`, closed) has no project doc describing its
   INTENDED USE — when to author a workflow vs. a bespoke operation, the YAML DSL, the human review/edit
   pattern, the prompt-library + eval seam. Knowledge is scattered across `README` (packaging),
   `docs/llm-framework.md` (agent-step angle), `CLAUDE.md` (MCP tools), and the `a88f` tickets. There is no
   `docs/workflow-engine.md`.
2. **LLM functionality is fragmented across two patterns.** `review_ticket`/`review_code`/`scan_spec` were
   reframed as workflows (WS-K2), but `verify_completion` (completion-verifier) was NOT, and the plan-review
   gate (`5fd2`) is currently specced as a bespoke orchestrator "mirroring verify_completion." LLM review
   logic therefore lives in BOTH the engine AND bespoke operations — the engine should be the single substrate.
3. **A core requirement was deferred.** BIDIRECTIONAL visual workflow editing was a core user requirement;
   `a88f` shipped only a read-only Mermaid render and explicitly deferred a bidirectional editor (story WS-I,
   "out of scope"). That deferral contradicts the expressed intent and must be revisited — but we should
   almost certainly NOT build a canvas editor ourselves; adopt a workflow FORMAT/technology with an
   established ecosystem of visual-editing tools.
4. **Prompt storage is misaligned.** The plan-review gate stores its criteria/prompts as bespoke
   `criteria_v*.json` descriptors under `docs/experiments/`, NOT in the engine's git-native prompt library
   (`resolve_prompt` / prompts-as-files / Langfuse prompt-management + the Inspect/promptfoo eval seam).

## Scope (workstreams — decomposed AFTER the brainstorm)

- **WS-DOC — usage documentation.** Author `docs/workflow-engine.md`: intended use; when to use a workflow
  vs. a bespoke op; the YAML DSL + schema; author/validate/render/run; scripted vs. agentic steps; the
  prompt-library + eval seam; and **the three-tier (three-pass) LLM-review gating pattern** (Pass-1 evidence
  records → Pass-2 independent binary-verify → Pass-3 deterministic gate; from epic `9da1`). This doc is the
  canonical anchor the review epics (`5fd2`/`9da1`) point at.
- **WS-MIGRATE — one substrate for all LLM functionality.** Migrate `verify_completion` onto the engine
  (revisiting `9da1`'s explicit out-of-scope hold); build the plan-review gate (`5fd2`) ON the engine, not
  as a bespoke orchestrator; confirm `review_ticket`/`review_code`/`scan_spec` parity. CAVEAT: the engine is
  a thin LINEAR executor (graphlib) — the gate's dynamic fan-out (Pass-1 → N findings → N Pass-2 verifier
  calls) + registry-driven facet-chunking may exceed the DSL; resolve whether the DSL gains map/fan-out
  steps or the orchestration stays scripted inside steps.
- **WS-PROMPTS — prompt storage on the engine.** Move the plan-review criteria (the `criteria_v*.json`
  scenarios = prompts) onto the engine's prompt-library / `resolve_prompt` / prompts-as-files + the eval
  seam, so criteria are versioned, evaluable, and visually reviewable like other workflow prompts.
- **WS-VISUAL — bidirectional visual editing via an established ecosystem.** Decide a workflow
  format/technology whose ecosystem ALREADY provides visual (ideally bidirectional) editing — rather than
  building our own canvas. Candidates to weigh in the brainstorm (NOT decided): BPMN (bpmn.io / Camunda
  Modeler), n8n, Node-RED, Argo Workflows, draw.io/diagrams.net round-trip, a Mermaid-edit round-trip, or a
  JSON/YAML graph with an off-the-shelf editor. CONSTRAINT: preserve clean git diffs + schema-validated text
  (the `a88f` invariant).
- **WS-VALIDATE — capabilities vs alternatives.** Re-evaluate the engine's capabilities against alternatives
  to confirm we built the right thing: Langflow (rejected in `a88f` — re-confirm), Burr (the `a88f`
  tripwire), Temporal/Prefect/Dagster, LangGraph-as-engine, n8n/Node-RED, Argo. Output: a kept-or-pivot
  recommendation.
- **WS-VISUAL-EVAL (candidate) — visual prompt + eval review.** Today Langfuse (optional, when keys set)
  gives a prompt-management + trace UI and `[eval]` wires Inspect/promptfoo (their own viewers), but there
  is no first-class, documented surface for humans to visually review prompts + eval STATUS together. Decide
  whether to make this first-class (Langfuse / Inspect / promptfoo, or other), and whether `a88f`'s
  "Langfuse = trace sink only" scoping should widen.
- **WS-INTENT-ALIGN (during this epic — NOT now).** The orchestrator working this epic should LOCATE the
  session log where the workflow engine was DESIGNED (candidate: the `a88f` implementation log
  `2eb5-1cdb-daaa-4655`; there may be an earlier design/brainstorm log) and align what was BUILT against the
  intent the user expressed there — specifically the visual-editing requirement — to ground WS-VISUAL and
  WS-VALIDATE.

## Initial thoughts & concerns (to seed the brainstorm)

- **Process failure — a CORE requirement was silently deferred.** Bidirectional visual editing was
  downgraded to "out of scope" in a story without surfacing the conflict with the user's stated intent —
  the same class of failure as silently descoping the completion-verifier migration.
- **Review-gate blind spot (carry to `5fd2` / `9da1`).** Our plan-review gates operate on the plan AS
  WRITTEN. They catch internal contradictions (COH), children-not-covering-the-parent's-ACs (G3), and
  body-doing-less-than-the-title (E3) — but ONLY when the requirement is recorded in the plan. They CANNOT
  catch an LLM silently descoping a requirement the USER expressed EXTERNALLY (in conversation/brainstorm)
  that was never written into the goal/ACs — there is no artifact of the original intent to compare against.
  Goal↔AC comparison catches it only when a broadly-stated goal visibly conflicts with a carve-out AC
  ("migrate ALL reviewers" vs "except completion_verifier"); it does NOT catch the visual-editing case,
  where the goal ITSELF was already narrowed to "read-only render." The missing capability is an
  **intent-provenance / requirement-traceability check** comparing the plan against an EXTERNAL intent source
  (the linked design session log / a requirements record) — exactly the WS-INTENT-ALIGN move. Candidate new
  plan-review criterion: **"intent-source fidelity."**
- **Don't reinvent.** WS-VISUAL must resist building a canvas editor; prefer an established format + ecosystem
  (the gate's own "reinventing-the-wheel" / "Frankenstein" antipatterns, applied to ourselves).
- **Thin-linear-executor tension.** The engine is deliberately a thin linear executor; review pipelines want
  fan-out. Resolve whether that is a DSL extension or a scripted-step concern before committing.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A canonical `docs/workflow-engine.md` documents intended use + the three-tier LLM-review gating pattern,
      and the review epics (`5fd2`/`9da1`) point at it.
- [ ] All LLM functionality (`verify_completion` + the plan-review gate + the existing reviewers) runs on the
      one workflow-engine substrate, with prompts in the engine's prompt library.
- [ ] A decision (with rationale) on bidirectional visual editing via an established ecosystem — adopted, or
      explicitly deferred again WITH the user's sign-off.
- [ ] A documented capability-vs-alternatives validation (kept-or-pivot).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/workflow-engine.md` exists: intended use, when-to-use-a-workflow, DSL + schema,
      author/validate/render/run, scripted-vs-agentic steps, prompt-library + eval seam, and the three-tier
      gating pattern.
- [ ] `verify_completion` migrated onto the workflow engine (revisiting the `9da1` out-of-scope decision);
      existing reviewers at parity; the plan-review gate built ON the engine.
- [ ] Plan-review criteria/prompts migrated onto the engine's prompt-storage + eval seam (off the bespoke
      `criteria_v*.json` experiment artifacts).
- [ ] A capability-vs-alternatives review (Langflow / Burr / Temporal / Prefect / Dagster / LangGraph / n8n /
      Argo) with a kept-or-pivot recommendation.
- [ ] A bidirectional-visual-editing decision grounded in an established format/ecosystem (not a bespoke
      canvas), preserving clean git diffs + schema validation.
- [ ] The workflow-engine DESIGN session log located and built-vs-intent aligned (the deferred visual-editing
      reconciliation).
- [ ] A candidate "intent-source fidelity" review criterion captured for `5fd2` / `9da1` (compare the plan
      against the external intent source).
- [ ] A visual prompt + eval-status review decision (Langfuse / Inspect / promptfoo, or other) — first-class
      or explicitly out.
- [ ] Brainstorm-first: a deep-dive design session precedes implementation; this epic is scope + concerns
      only until then.
data.title: Workflow-engine remediation: unify LLM functionality on the engine, restore visual editing, document intended use
